Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Appendix
Off Calendar Oct-23-2009 Text Ruling

Petitioners Luis Aljejo And Maria Medina'S Amended Petitioners' Motion For Preliminary Injunction (Continued From 10/20/09 To 10/22/09)
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petitn's' Amended Petition for motion for prelim. injunc.. Appearances: Cynthia L. Rice, Esq. of California Rural Legal Assistance (415) 777-2752 x 323, appearing for Petitn's and moving party; Deborah Escobedo, Esq. Youth Law Center, (415) 543-3379, x3907 also, appearing for Petitn's. G. Mateo Munoz, Deputy Atty. Gen. , Dept. of Justice, (916) 327-7855 appearing for Respondent, O'Connell, et al; Christine Murphy, Office of Atty. Gen., Dept. of Education, (916) 327-6750; appearing for Resp's Dept. of Education, State Bd. Of Ed., Supertn. O'Connell. Argued & the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for prelim. injunc. denied. Where mandamus is available, the equitable remedy of an injunction is ordinarily improper. Moore vs. Superior Court (1936) 6 Cal.2nd 421. Insufficient showing of imminent or irreparable harm. Dftn. is still employing programs other than on-site monitoring to assist LEP students and is not abandoning them. No showing plaintiff likely to prevail on the merits. Insufficient showing that Education Code section 52177(d) (three year on-site monitoring cycle) is still operative in view of sunset provision of EC section 62000.2(c). EC section 62002 specifies the relevant statutes adopted regarding the use of funds shall not be operative except as specified in EC section 62002.5, which does not mention the continuation of on-site monitoring. Dftn. has broad discretion to establish the process and frequency of on-site reviews. The EEO Act does not specifically require on-site monitoring and the evidence shows Dftn. is still taking steps to protect LEP students while trying to fine-tune the monitoring program. Pltn. is also attempting to change the status quo by seeking a three year monitoring cycle where there has historically only been a four year cycle. Evidence insufficient re: sunsetting provision. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Lertora CRS 11935

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Aug-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Respondents Jack O'Connell, in his official capacity as the State Superintendent of Public Instruction, the State Board of Education; Sophia Angelis, James D. Aschwanden, Rae Belisle, Alan Bersin, Ruth Bloom, Yvonne Chan, Gregory W. Jones, David P. Lopez, Jorge Lopez, Theodore R. Mitchell, Johnathan Xavier Williams, in their official capacities as members of the State Board of Education, the California Department of Education, and the State of California Demurrer to Petitioners' Petition for Writ of Mandate. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the State and the individual members of the State Board of Education. (See State of California vs. Superior Court (1974) 12 Cal.3d 237, 255-256.) Demurrer overruled as to the remainder. The Court reads the petition liberally (CCP section 452.) Petitioner alleged no monitoring is taking place. Education Code section 52177 appears to be enforceable and requires "monitoring." Plaintiff properly plead standing under CCP section 526a, taxpayer standing for illegal expenditure/diversion of funds. Cause of action for declaratory relief appropriate. (CCP section 1060.) Ten days leave to answer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
